source.notes: Centralized user-facing language for the Guided Parcel Review static site. Dynamic property, tax, market, and statistical values remain in data/model files.

site.description: A guided parcel review that helps taxpayers understand property records, assessed value, tax context, market context, equalization, and review items.
site.loadErrorTitle: Guided Parcel Review could not load

routes.label: Property Record
routes.question: Does the county record describe this property correctly?
routes.title: Review the property record before value or taxes
routes.description: Start with identity, land, building facts, and photos.

routes.label: What Changed
routes.question: What changed?
routes.title: Look at the value movement
routes.description: Compare the latest change with the longer value history.

routes.label: Value Detail
routes.question: What may be driving the value?
routes.title: Compare the value with the property record and local sales
routes.description: Read the property facts beside recent qualified sales.

routes.label: Equalization
routes.question: Are assessments checked for level and consistency?
routes.title: Use equalization to check the assessment base
routes.description: Equalization checks whether assessments meet required levels and are applied consistently.

routes.label: State Context
routes.question: How does the county compare statewide?
routes.title: Keep the statewide comparison inside the tax context step
routes.description: Use statewide CTL data as broader context.

routes.label: Tax Context
routes.question: How do values connect to taxes?
routes.title: See how value changes affect the tax bill
routes.description: Review value, levy, credits, districts, and net tax separately.

routes.label: Review Signals
routes.question: Is there anything worth reviewing more closely?
routes.title: Review neutral signals and organize follow-up questions
routes.description: Check whether the source facts line up.

routes.label: Summary
routes.question: What was reviewed?
routes.title: Leave with orientation and optional next steps
routes.description: Review the record, value, equalization, taxes, and signals in one place.

start.title: Preview the review workspace
start.intro: Each sample opens a full parcel view with record details, value history, tax context, market charts, and guided review steps.
start.calloutText: Residential, agricultural, and commercial samples are available.

cards.title: Parcel context
cards.description: Review parcel facts, classification, land details, valuation groups, and practical items to verify.

cards.title: Value and assessment history
cards.description: See how the sample property's assessed value has moved and which years are still pending or finalized.

cards.title: Tax impact
cards.description: See how value changes, levy, credits, and effective tax rate relate to the latest available tax bill.

start.disclaimer: This prototype uses pre-loaded sample records for demonstration, stress testing, and smoke testing. Official records, valuations, and tax determinations remain with the appropriate county offices.

directStart.title: Start with this property
directStart.intro: This link is ready to open a specific property review. First, review the short notice so the property view is read as guidance, not an official record.
directStart.calloutText: The linked property will open after you acknowledge the informational notice.

cards.title: Parcel context
cards.description: Review parcel facts, classification, land details, valuation groups, and practical items to verify.

cards.title: Value and assessment history
cards.description: See how the property's assessed value has moved and which years are still pending or finalized.

cards.title: Tax impact
cards.description: See how value changes, levy, credits, and effective tax rate relate to the latest available tax bill.

directStart.disclaimer: This prototype uses pre-loaded records for demonstration, stress testing, and smoke testing. Official records, valuations, and tax determinations remain with the appropriate county offices.

transitions.intro: Start with the record facts. These details anchor the value, market, equalization, and tax views that follow.
transitions.handoff: You have completed the first step: reviewing your property details. The green button takes you to the next step, where you can learn more about what may have changed from year to year.

propertyDetails.title: Property details
propertyDetails.description: These property details shape the value and tax views that follow. Reviewing them first makes the later numbers easier to follow.

decisionCheck.title: Does the record look right?
decisionCheck.body: Review these facts against what you know about the property. Follow up if the owner, address, land size, class, condition, rooms, garage, improvements, or sketch appears inaccurate or incomplete.

transitions.intro: Start with the current assessment and the latest change.
transitions.history: Now compare the current change with prior years.
transitions.handoff: You have reviewed the current assessment and recent value history. Next, look closer at the value details behind the change.

notice.title: Prior & Current Assessments
notice.description: Review land, building, other improvements, and total value before connecting those numbers to taxes.

transitions.intro: Start with the qualified sales behind this property class. The count and distribution help explain the measures below.
transitions.source: Next, place the property in its local comparison group. These local sales-study measures introduce the countywide equalization check.
transitions.scatter: With the sales base in view, compare this property group with nearby groups. The scatterplot shows how the groups relate to each other.
transitions.handoff: You have reviewed the sales base and the property's local comparison group. Next, move to the countywide equalization layer.

priceContext.title: What prices are represented?
priceContext.description: Average sale price, average assessed value, and level of value show price context for the selected group.

transitions.intro: Qualified sales from the sales-study period test assessed values against market evidence. Those sales produce the statistical measures below.
transitions.unified: Next, compare the measures on one scale. The chart shows whether COD, PRD, COV, and level of value move together or diverge.
transitions.placementTitle: Assessment places the property in the shared value base.
transitions.placementBody: Equalization checks that the shared value base is uniform and proportionate before levies and taxes are applied.
transitions.placementEmphasis: Next, carry that shared value base into tax context, where levies and credits turn assessed value into the bill.

transitions.intro: Start with the tax district and levy mix. The chart shows where the latest gross levy is allocated before the history view.
transitions.history: Next, read the completed statement years. The table tracks levy, credits, net tax, and effective tax rate; the chart shows the rate pattern.
transitions.calculation: Then isolate the latest statement math. This shorthand shows how assessed value and levy become gross tax, then how credits reduce it to net tax.
transitions.pattern: After the statement math, isolate the net-bill pattern. This view shows where the bill peaked and how the latest statement compares with the period average.
transitions.baseline: Step back to the county baseline. Countywide value, tax, and rate trends show the broader tax context before the optional statewide comparison.
transitions.handoff: You have reviewed how the tax bill is built from levies, credits, and the county baseline. Next, bring the record, value, equalization, and tax signals together.

your-taxes.taxPatternNote: Dashed line: period average. Points use statement net tax after credits.

countyBaseline.title: Countywide baseline
countyBaseline.intro: Certified values and taxes levied show the broader value base and the public obligations distributed across it before the parcel-level tax step.
countyBaseline.valueTitle: How are county values and taxes moving?
countyBaseline.valueDescription: Values and taxes are indexed to the same starting point so their movement can be compared over time.
countyBaseline.rateTitle: How do county taxes compare with value?
countyBaseline.rateDescription: The countywide effective tax rate divides taxes levied by certified value, giving a broad tax-rate baseline.
countyBaseline.source: Source: 2019-2025 Nebraska Certificates of Taxes Levied (CTL).

advancedContext.title: Want a broader comparison?
advancedContext.body: View this county with statewide CTL patterns and other Nebraska counties without leaving the main review path.

comparison.title: How does this county compare?
comparison.description: This comparison uses the assessment-year range shown in the charts below. It starts with a simple baseline: Nebraska equals 100. A county above 100 has a higher average tax rate than the statewide average; below 100 has a lower rate. Certified values, taxes levied, and average tax rates provide additional context.
comparison.decisionBody: The Nebraska = 100 comparison, value growth, tax growth, and average rate movement show whether the local pattern is unusual or broadly typical.
comparison.indexedTitle: How do value and tax growth compare?
comparison.rateTitle: How do average tax rates compare?
comparison.source: Source: 2019-2025 Nebraska Certificates of Taxes Levied (CTL).

statewide.transition: The statewide baseline is supporting context.
statewide.title: What statewide pattern is behind that comparison?
statewide.description: After the county comparison, the statewide CTL baseline shows the broader movement in values, taxes levied, and average tax rates.
statewide.indexedTitle: How are statewide values and taxes moving?
statewide.indexedDescription: Statewide values and taxes are indexed to 2019 so their movement can be compared over time.
statewide.rateTitle: How is the statewide average tax rate trending?
statewide.rateDescription: The statewide average tax rate is a broad reference point for understanding local tax patterns.
statewide.source: Source: 2019-2025 Nebraska Certificates of Taxes Levied (CTL).
statewide.handoff: You have reviewed the county comparison and statewide baseline. You can return to tax context for the view you came from, or move ahead to review signals.

resourceShell.title: Resources and policies
resourceShell.description: Need help with what you just reviewed? Find focused answers and forms.

about.title: About Guided Parcel Review
about.body: Guided Parcel Review walks through one property in a clear sequence: record, value, market context, equalization, taxes, and review signals.

terms.title: Use of this site
notes: This site is provided for public information and general guidance. It is not a legal notice, tax bill, valuation decision, or substitute for official records.
Information may be incomplete, pending, delayed, or updated after publication. Official county, state, court, or agency records control when accuracy, deadlines, rights, or obligations matter.
Links to outside sites are provided for convenience. Those sites have their own terms, privacy practices, forms, and instructions. Use this site lawfully and do not rely on it as professional advice.

privacy.title: Privacy and public information
notes: This site may log basic, cookie-free visit events, such as which linked parcel page opened, which review steps were viewed, approximate time on page, and whether resource links were selected.
If you submit information through a form, email, or support channel, it may be used to respond to your request and may be subject to applicable public-records, retention, or disclosure laws.

This site should not sell personal information. Information may be shared with service providers, official agencies, or others when needed to operate the site, respond to requests, or comply with law.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.body: This demonstration is intended to be readable, keyboard-accessible, and usable with assistive technology. Before public release, it should receive formal accessibility review, assistive-technology testing, and color-contrast verification.

contact.title: Contact and support
contact.body: For questions about official records, valuation, exemptions, levies, or payment, contact the appropriate county or state office. This demonstration does not replace official guidance.

recordCorrection.title: Report a property record discrepancy
recordCorrection.description: Use this form to request factual record review when parcel, land, dwelling, improvement, or other property details appear inaccurate, incomplete, or misclassified.

faqs: What should I check first?
Start with ownership, situs address, legal description, dwelling facts, improvements, condition, and photos. Factual record issues can affect later value review.
Do bedrooms, bathrooms, condition, and square footage matter?
Yes. Those characteristics help describe the property and can influence the assessment model.
What if a photo or property characteristic looks outdated?
Use the record review request to describe what appears inaccurate, incomplete, or misclassified.
Is assessed value the same as market value?
For most residential real property, assessed value is intended to reflect market value as of the assessment date.
Why are land and improvement values separated?
Separating land from buildings helps show which part of the property model changed.
Why did the value change?
Value can change because of updated property facts, market movement, depreciation, new construction, or sale evidence.
How does market evidence matter?
Sales evidence helps explain whether assessments are moving with the market.
Why does tax context come after equalization?
Equalization checks whether assessments are at the required level and applied consistently. Tax context then shows how levies, credits, exemptions, and tax-district assignment affect the bill.
What is the difference between gross tax and net tax?
Gross tax starts from value and levy. Net tax reflects applicable credits and adjustments before payment status is applied.
Why do value and taxes not always move together?
Taxes also depend on budgets, levies, exemptions, credits, and tax district changes.
What does effective tax rate show?
It divides statement net tax by assessed value so different years can be compared more clearly.
Where do credits fit?
Credits reduce the final amount due after the tax calculation is applied.
What is a market area?
It is a group of properties reviewed together because they share market or valuation characteristics.
Are nearby sales exact matches?
Usually no. Sales are market evidence because they are reasonably related to the same local market.
What do local sales trends show?
They help explain whether values are moving with nearby market evidence.
What is a ratio study?
It compares assessed values with sale prices to test assessment level and uniformity.
Why does equalization sit before taxes?
It checks whether assessments are at the required level and applied consistently before levies are applied.
What does equalization not do?
It does not stop market values from moving, set the tax levy, or decide whether one parcel outcome is right or wrong.
What do COD and PRD measure?
COD describes assessment uniformity. PRD helps flag whether high- and low-value properties are treated consistently.
Can countywide measures prove the parcel value is wrong?
Not by themselves. They are context; parcel facts and market evidence still matter.
What does the Property Assessment Division do?
Nebraska's Property Assessment Division helps frame statewide assessment oversight and comparison.
How do state assessment reports help?
They summarize county sales studies and equalization information used for statewide context.
What is statewide equalization?
It is the state-level role of reviewing whether county assessments meet required standards.
Are credits decided here?
No. Official credits and tax calculations are applied through the tax process.
What should I review first?
Start with the property record: square footage, year built, basement, garage, outbuildings, condition, lot size, property class, value history, and tax history.
What should I do with an unresolved question?
Keep notes, verify official source documents, and contact the appropriate county or state office if the issue depends on official records or deadlines.
What steps feed these signals?
They come from the property record, value movement, equalization measures, tax context, and source documents.
Does this summary decide an outcome?
No. It is a review aid. It separates property facts, value movement, taxes, and context.

categories.title: Ownership & mailing details
categories.description: Names, addresses, parcel identifiers, and other administrative details tied to the property record.

categories.title: Land & site details
categories.description: Land size, site characteristics, zoning, and classification details that describe the parcel itself.

categories.title: Dwelling / main structure
categories.description: The main building details shown on the record, including size, age, layout, quality, and major systems.

categories.title: Garages & attached improvements
categories.description: Attached or closely related improvements that may be listed separately from the main structure.

categories.title: Outbuildings & farm/ag structures
categories.description: Detached buildings and agricultural structures that may appear as separate improvement records.

categories.title: Other record details
categories.description: Anything unusual or important that does not fit neatly into the other review areas.

recordCorrectionRequest.title: Property Record Correction Request
recordCorrectionRequest.acknowledgment: I understand this request is for factual property record review.

emailBody.attachedTemplate: Attached is a property record correction request for parcel {parcelId}.
emailBody.contactTemplate: Please contact me by {preferredContact} if additional information is needed.

pdf.intro: Use this request for factual review of parcel, land, dwelling, improvement, or other property record details.
pdf.emptyCategories: No review categories selected; comments provided.
